title: Marcus and the Lost Baby Duck
synopsis: Marcus, a kind little boy, discovers a sad baby duck who has lost his family at the park pond. With courage and determination, Marcus helps the duckling find his way back to his mama and siblings, learning about being brave and helping others.

pages.text: Marcus loved going to the park with his mama. Today, the sun was bright and warm. Marcus had beautiful brown skin and curly black hair. He wore his favorite red shirt with a smiling sun on it.

illustration.description: A cheerful African American boy with curly black hair and warm brown skin wearing a bright red t-shirt with a yellow sun design. He's walking hand-in-hand with his mother, a woman with natural hair in a colorful headwrap, through a sunny park with green grass and tall trees. The style is bright cartoon with bold primary colors and rounded, friendly shapes.
illustration.alt: Marcus walking with his mama in the park

pages.text: Near the blue pond, Marcus heard a tiny sound. "Peep! Peep!" It was a small yellow duckling. The baby duck looked very sad. His little head hung down low, and he made worried peeping sounds.

illustration.description: A small, fluffy yellow duckling sitting alone by a bright blue pond, looking downcast with his head drooping. Marcus is crouching nearby, his expression showing concern and curiosity. The pond has lily pads and cattails, with a textured background resembling construction paper in soft blues and greens.
illustration.alt: A sad little duckling by the pond with Marcus nearby

pages.text: "What's wrong, little duck?" Marcus asked gently. The duckling looked up with big, scared eyes. "Peep! Peep!" he cried. Marcus felt sad too. He knew the baby duck needed help. Marcus wanted to be brave and kind.

illustration.description: Marcus kneeling down at eye level with the small yellow duckling, his face showing gentle concern and determination. The duckling has large, expressive dark eyes looking up hopefully. Behind them, diverse families enjoy the park - an Asian family having a picnic and a Latino father pushing his daughter on a swing. Bright, warm colors dominate the scene.
illustration.alt: Marcus talking gently to the scared duckling

pages.text: Marcus's mama came over. "The baby duck is lost," Marcus told her. "He can't find his family." Mama smiled proudly. "You have a kind heart, Marcus. Let's help him together." Marcus felt happy and brave now.

illustration.description: Marcus's mother, a kind woman with natural hair in a bright African print headwrap, crouching beside Marcus and the duckling. Her expression is warm and encouraging. Marcus looks determined and happy, standing tall with his shoulders back. The duckling sits between them, looking slightly more hopeful with his head raised a bit higher.
illustration.alt: Marcus and his mama deciding to help the duckling together

pages.text: Marcus and Mama walked around the pond. They looked behind the big rocks and under the bridge. "Quack! Quack!" they heard. It was Mama Duck with three other baby ducklings! She looked worried and was calling loudly.

illustration.description: Marcus and his mother searching near a small stone bridge over the pond. In the distance, a white and brown mama duck with three yellow ducklings can be seen swimming, with the mama duck's neck stretched up calling. The scene has a sense of movement and discovery, with cattails and water lilies adding natural details to the bright blue water.
illustration.alt: Marcus and his mama finding the duck family by the bridge

pages.text: "Look!" Marcus pointed. "There's his family!" The baby duck heard his mama's call and got very excited. "Peep! Peep!" he chirped happily. Marcus felt so proud. He had been brave and helped solve the problem. The little duck waddled fast to his mama.

illustration.description: The joyful reunion scene showing the baby duckling running toward his family while Marcus points and smiles widely. Mama Duck has her wings spread welcomingly, and the three sibling ducklings are swimming in a circle excitedly. Marcus has his arms raised in celebration, his face beaming with happiness and pride. Golden sunlight sparkles on the water.
illustration.alt: Happy reunion as the baby duck returns to his family

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did the baby duck feel when Marcus first found him?
questions.explanation: The baby duck was sad and scared because he was lost and couldn't find his family.

questions.question: What did Marcus want to do when he saw the sad duckling?
questions.explanation: Marcus was kind and wanted to help the lost baby duck find his family.

questions.question: How did Marcus feel when he helped the duckling?
questions.explanation: Marcus felt proud and happy because he was brave and helped solve the duckling's problem.

questions.question: Where did they find the duck family?
choices: By the bridge at the pond
questions.explanation: Mama Duck and the other baby ducks were by the bridge, calling for their lost sibling.

blanks.text: Marcus heard the baby duck say 'Peep! ____!'
The duckling looked very ____ when Marcus found him.
Marcus wanted to be ____ and help the duckling.
They found Mama Duck and three other ____ ducklings.
